intro: Skottsbergiella fernandezia is a species of plant in the family Santalaceae, native to the Juan Fernández Islands off the coast of Chile. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The bioactive constituents of Skottsbergiella fernandezia include terpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Skottsbergiella fernandezia is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, such as colds, fever, and skin conditions.
